response: Given that \((\mathbf{a} - \mathbf{b}) \cdot [(\mathbf{b} - \mathbf{c}) \times (\mathbf{c} - \mathbf{a})]\), we first compute the vectors \(\mathbf{a} - \mathbf{b}, \mathbf{b} - \mathbf{c}, \mathbf{c} - \mathbf{a}\). \[\mathbf{a} - \mathbf{b} = \begin{pmatrix} -3 - 5 \\ 10 - \pi \\ 1 - 0 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} -8 \\ 10 - \pi \\ 1 \end{pmatrix} \]. \[ \mathbf{b} - \mathbf{c} = \begin{pmatrix} 5 - (-2) \\ \pi - (-2) \\ 0 - 7 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 7 \\ \pi + 2 \\ -7 \end{pmatrix}. \] \[ \mathbf{c} - \mathbf{a} = \begin{pmatrix} -2 - (-3) \\ -2 - 10 \\ 7 - 1 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ -12 \\ 6 \end{pmatrix}. \] Next, we compute the cross product \((\mathbf{b} - \mathbf{c}) \times (\mathbf{c} - \mathbf{a})\). \[ \begin{pmatrix} 7 \\ \pi + 2 \\ -7 \end{pmatrix} \times \begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ -12 \\ 6 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} (\pi + 2)(6) - (-7)(-12) \\ -7(1) - 7(6) \\ 7(-12) - (\pi + 2)(1) \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 6\pi + 12 - 84 \\ -7 - 42 \\ -84 - \pi - 2 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 6\pi - 72 \\ -49 \\ -\pi - 86 \end{pmatrix}. \] Finally, we compute the dot product \((\mathbf{a} - \mathbf{b}) \cdot [(\mathbf{b} - \mathbf{c}) \times (\mathbf{c} - \mathbf{a})]\). \[ \begin{pmatrix} -8 \\ 10 - \pi \\ 1 \end{pmatrix} \cdot \begin{pmatrix} 6\pi - 72 \\ -49 \\ -\pi - 86 \end{pmatrix} = -8(6\pi - 72) + (10 - \pi)(-49) + 1(-\pi - 86) = -48\pi + 576 - 490 + 49\pi - \pi - 86 = -10\pi - 10. \] The final answer is \(-10\pi - 10\). </think> <answer> -10\pi - 10 </answer>
